channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: The America First Contradiction
item.description: A Pentagon official’s X thread exposes the cognitive dissonance of Trump’s foreign policy.

item.title: Zelensky’s Cabinet Reshuffle Sparks Public Outcry in Ukraine
item.description: Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov was popular for his drone development push—but not enough to keep him in office.

item.title: Russia is losing its grip on Crimea
item.description: Ukraine’s drone offensive is making it harder to supply and defend the vital peninsula

item.title: Where Will Global Financial Fragmentation Lead?
item.description: Debates about geopolitical fragmentation always revive the late economist Robert Mundell’s dream of a single world money. But participants should be mindful of the euro’s real-world experience over the past quarter-century.

item.title: German Military Power Needs to Go It Alone
item.description: NATO’s multinational forces are an unwieldy relic of a more peaceful past.

item.title: Why Gen Z Is Back on the Streets in Bangladesh
item.description: A controversy centered on Bangladesh's Higher Secondary Certificate (HSC) exam brought students back out to protest.

item.title: Development Economics for an Age of Upheaval
item.description: The framework that has long guided development economics no longer reflects how modern economies actually create value. As the boundaries that once separated sectors become blurred, prosperity increasingly depends on how economies build the ecosystems that sustain technological progress and nurture new productive capacity.

item.title: A Divided World Is North Korea’s Greatest Asset
item.description: Geopolitical fragmentation has hamstrung nuclear sanctions regimes. Pyongyang is taking advantage.

item.title: Why China Will Not Sell 5th-Generation J-35 Fighter Jets to Pakistan (Yet)
item.description: For China, the decision to sell the J-35 would carry ramifications far exceeding those of any previous Chinese arms sale.

item.title: The Gap Between Reform and Local Power in Uzbekistan
item.description: One woman’s attempt to stand for election as mahalla chairperson reveals the barriers that remain to inclusive self-government in Uzbekistan.

item.title: Nigeria Is Quietly Becoming a Diplomatic Powerhouse
item.description: Many analysts think Abuja’s foreign policy is on the decline. Here’s why that view is misguided.

item.title: Cartoon: Continuing uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A lighter take on the news

item.title: Israel’s future hangs in the balance
item.description: The country’s coming election may be its most important ever

item.title: America should stop making it so hard to have fun
item.description: Bureaucracy is nightlife’s toughest bouncer

item.title: China’s rulers have a woman problem
item.description: Antagonism between the sexes does nothing to address China’s demographic problems

item.title: A real but selective crackdown on Cambodia’s scam industry
item.description: Sanctions from America and pressure from China have forced a reckoning

item.title: China wants to end AI romances
item.description: It was all having too much impact on young people’s lives

item.title: Lindsey Graham tried to save the wrong countries from Donald Trump
item.description: He should have put American democracy first

item.title: Democrats challenge a big Hollywood tie-up
item.description: A dozen states are suing to block Paramount’s merger with Warner Bros

item.title: Business
Politics
In praise of Scandinavia’s risky and dirty playgrounds
item.description: Kids stand to gain from a few bumps, bruises and mud-splatters

item.title: Sweden squashed migration. The populist right wants to go further
item.description: The Sweden Democrats could be heading for a bigger role in government

item.title: Opening the border between Spain and Gibraltar
item.description: The deal offers hope for a more pragmatic relationship between Britain and Europe

item.title: Why Berlin’s airport is still completely hopeless
item.description: Blame passengers, lobbying, history and geography

item.title: Europe seems set to ease its carbon pricing
item.description: Green goals are running into fears about competitiveness

item.title: How SK Hynix became the king of advanced memory chips
item.description: Its advantages will not protect it if demand falters

item.title: How to sell a kettle
item.description: Brand positioning reaches boiling point

item.title: A hit new show takes on polygamy in modern South Africa
item.description: “The Polygamist” is trashy yet perceptive about family, patriarchy and inequality

item.title: America’s other battle with Iran
item.description: The two countries are vying for influence in Iraq

item.title: The Panama Canal is growing more important
item.description: And the challenges to it are getting more acute

item.title: The biggest schools training America’s doctors are in the Caribbean
item.description: The industry has a seedy underbelly

item.title: Playing for a Place in the World Through Taiwan’s Baseball Diplomacy
item.description: International sport offers something that conventional diplomacy often cannot: a stage on which Taiwan can appear before a global audience.

item.title: The Death of the Degree
item.description: AI is often portrayed as a threat to higher education, with some administrators and professors focused on rooting out its use. But this approach fails to recognize that, in an AI-transformed world, the new dividing lines are technical capabilities and institutional pipelines that can quickly transform research into products.

item.title: Hungary’s Real Economic Test
item.description: Entering the euro with favorable market sentiment, but without an economy that can function well within a monetary union, would not serve Hungary’s long-term interests. It would be a formula for more broken promises—precisely the trend that the new government was elected to end.

item.title: An unreliable America is drawing Asia’s middle powers closer
item.description: A burst of Indian diplomacy exemplifies the trend

item.title: Sovereign AI, independent of America and China, is a pipe dream
item.description: But a degree of protection from coercion remains possible

item.title: Ukraine’s reformist defence minister is ousted
item.description: The generals pushed back at his efforts to modernise the army

item.title: Donald Trump’s transformation of the Department of Justice will be hard to undo
item.description: Political prosecutions are just part of a more radical reinvention

item.title: How to shrink the Fed’s $7trn balance-sheet
item.description: Kevin Warsh will struggle to reverse the effects of bond-buying

item.title: Donald Trump’s gutting of the Department of Justice
item.description: What the degraded institution means for America

item.title: It’s too darn hot. Blame global dimming
item.description: Earth is absorbing a lot more sunshine

item.title: Can Kevin Warsh’s Fed force 5 reimagine monetary policymaking?
item.description: The new chairman enlists heavy-hitters to fight a handful of gnarly problems

item.title: How Social Media Posts Are Fueling the Dehumanization of Malaysia’s Rohingya Population
item.description: The growing negative sentiment about the community bears a disturbing resemblance to the narratives that justified their mass expulsion from Myanmar in 2017.

item.title: Bloomberg Ordered to Pay $357,000 in Damages After Losing Singapore Defamation Case
item.description: The High Court ruled that a 2024 article on the city-state's luxury real estate market maliciously defamed ministers Tan See Leng and K. Shanmugam.

item.title: How Water Is Becoming Pakistan’s Nuclear Threshold
item.description: The real danger lies not in the immediate scarcity of Indus water flowing from India into Pakistan but in the widening gap between how they interpret the same facts.

item.title: What the West Bengal Egg Row Says About Indian Society
item.description: The BJP government, whose support base includes traditionalist Hindus, moved to drop eggs from the midday meal program in Kolkata.

item.title: Iranian Attacks Need Not Change the Gulf’s AI Ambitions
item.description: Ukraine offers lessons in how to keep infrastructure alive in wartime.

item.title: The Coming Clash Between China and Europe
item.description: Why a trade war can’t be avoided.

item.title: The Lost Art of Coercion
item.description: Why Trump’s belligerence is a sign of weakness.

item.title: Myanmar Military and Some Rebel Groups Ready For Talks, Thai FM Says
item.description: ASEAN special envoy Ma. Theresa Lazaro held separate talks with military-backed negotiators and six rebel groups in Pattaya earlier this week.

item.title: How to make AI safe—and lessen dependence on America and China
item.description: State-backed efforts to catch up with frontier models are doomed
